lib/app/di/modules/offline_module.dart.changes: Registered SyncQueueDao and SyncQueueRepository dependencies, removed TODO comments, added detailed documentation for Phase 4 sync queue system

lib/features/offline/data/models/sync_op.changes: Added toDomainEntity() method for converting model to domain entity, enhancing domain-driven architecture

lib/features/offline/data/repositories/o.changes: Added export for sync_queue_repository_impl.dart to expose the new repository implementation

lib/features/offline/data/repositories/s.changes: New file implementing SyncQueueRepository with comprehensive queue management operations including enqueue, dequeue, query, status updates, cleanup, and statistics

lib/features/offline/domain/repositories.changes: Export file for domain repository interfaces
Interface file defining contract for sync queue repository operations

patterns_discovered: Domain-driven architecture with clear separation between domain entities, data models, and repositories
Dependency injection pattern using GetIt for managing singleton instances
Repository pattern abstraction for database operations
Comprehensive error handling with RepositoryException wrapper
SQLite ORM using Drift for persistent storage
Enum-based status management for sync operations
Batch operations support for performance optimization
gotchas_discovered: DAO initialization depends on DatabaseService rather than direct AppDatabase access
Repository layer throws RepositoryException for consistent error handling
Dequeue operation requires marking items as processing to prevent race conditions
Entity-to-model conversion pattern established with toDomainEntity() method
Companion objects used for Drift ORM data transformation
discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Successfully implemented comprehensive sync queue repository with all required operations including enqueue, dequeue, query, status management, cleanup, and statistics. The implementation follows clean architecture principles with proper separation of concerns and comprehensive error handling.
recommendations: Consider adding transaction support for atomic operations when enqueueing multiple items
Implement exponential backoff retry logic in the service layer above the repository
Add monitoring and logging for queue performance metrics
Consider implementing batch processing for large queue operations
Add validation for entity and entity IDs before repository operations
Implement queue size limits and overflow handling strategy
